title: hieracium norrliniiforme subsp. tephranthelum
side_effects: Hieracium norrliniiforme subsp. tephranthelum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. It may also cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. In severe cases, improper use of this plant can lead to more serious health complications, like liver or kidney damage, and even interact negatively with certain medications. If consumed in large quantities or over an extended period, it may also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue.
